creator: timido@ubuntu.com
text:
hey @koobs this is Aaron from @Freenode and Aaronfarias @ Libera

please do let me know the situation in how everything plays out so. I can register back the channel #freebsd-es and the channels. you will see me posting more things than the usual thank you for those who read this.

and as a community we should grow together instead of having bad vibes. meaning if there is a channel for example #FreeBSD and perhaps. I know the rules when it comes to spam but if my podcast is about Freebsd. Why there is always the bad negative OP who wants to make them selves standout from the crow....

I am not criticizing any @. I am very thankful for those who served  in the project directly. I want to help out more FreeBSD community specially with my IRC knowledge in dealing with bots, commands, and much more... I am not throwing my self to the mercy of the @'s. I Just want to say if we want to grow as a community so more users can enter our. IRC is always good if you are an op. Instead of fighting with normal users. Like me.... read the rules before giving out any type of warnings in the channel. I am not saying the responsibilities of an op @ is rewarding but if you are representing the project in some shape of form, at least show your professionalism not by fighting with the user, in. The channel /query the user and let them know. I think as an IRC community we should all be lay back. and I am not criticizing any OPS. if the user wants to speak of a port so users can READ why create 10000000 of Freebsd channels if we can only have a few so we can get directly to the. Point. 

Once Again I thanks all the administrators who manage IRC, Forums, and show my appreciation. in not by fighting. we should be a lay back community. I know we have a lot of talented and well educated it  folks. And before fighting with a user we should. Read and understand  the problem of the users so others can learn about the situation and not only learn but if they have questions they ask right away. God is love and we should all love each other for contributing to the community some way some how.

Sincerely @Aaron From Miami Florida
